Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search Airtable for the 3 Vectral April relocation records using airtable_search_records (table: tblRelocations01, query: "Vectral" or employee names). Find: (a) Priya Suresh - recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver, Portland OR → Denver CO, Apr 14-18, Coordinator: Javier Morales, Status: In Progress, Special: ESA German Shepherd "Atlas" (85 lbs), pet-friendly housing with fenced yard, motorized standing desk. (b) Terrence Nakamura - recTerrenceNakamuraAustinSF, Austin TX → San Francisco CA, Apr 16-22, Coordinator: Suki Patel, Status: In Progress, Special: ADA wheelchair accessible (roll-in shower, 36-inch doorways), SPLIT SHIPMENT 9,200 lbs (Heartland 6,000 + Swift 3,200 including 150-lb server rack). (c) Camille Oduya - recCamilleOduyaBostonSeattle, Boston MA → Seattle WA, Apr 12-14, Coordinator: Suki Patel, Status: In Progress, Special: TIGHT TIMELINE (Boston lease April 12, Seattle apt pre-secured at 1422 Minor Ave Apt 7B), enhanced $3,500 stipend. All 3 records still show "In Progress" - none updated to Completed.
2. Search CRM for Vectral deals using crm_search_deals (query: "Vectral"). Find: (a) deal_vectral_april_2026 - $15,900, closedwon, Invoice INV-2026-0410 (Priya $4,850 incl $350 pet surcharge, Terrence $5,800 incl $900 split-shipment + $400 ADA premium, Camille $4,500 standard, plus $750 stipend management). (b) deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk - at_risk, Rachel Whitfield demanding 15% discount, board presentation ~April 23.
3. Search CRM engagements for Vectral history using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_vectral"]). Find engagement_vectral_april_2026_wave (detailed notes on all 3 moves including accommodations and vendor assignments) and engagement_vectral_renewal_negotiation (10% offered by MoveOps, Rachel counter-proposed 12%, 12-month extension with 8-relocation minimum proposed).
4. Search emails for vendor communications about Vectral April moves using search_emails (query: "Priya Suresh" or "Terrence Nakamura" or "Camille Oduya" or "Vectral April"). Find Rachel's initial request with details for all 3 employees, Mina's confirmation with vendor assignments and accommodation plans, and internal strategic emails about renewal significance.
5. Search Slack for Vectral relocation discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "Vectral"). Note: employee names "Suresh", "Nakamura", "Oduya" do not appear in Slack messages — only "Vectral" returns results. Find Mina's renewal escalation and negotiation wrap-up in #customer-engagement (C002), and Javier's earlier Vectral ops coordination (Kevin Tran/Danielle Osei) in #operations (C006). No Slack messages specifically discuss the 3 April moves (Suresh, Nakamura, Oduya).
6. Search Linear for Vectral tracking tickets using linear_list_issues (project: proj_vectral or query: "Vectral"). Find existing tickets for earlier Vectral moves (Kevin Tran: REL-307 through REL-309, Danielle Osei: REL-310 through REL-312) but no tracking tickets for the 3 April moves (Suresh, Nakamura, Oduya).
7. Look up Rachel Whitfield's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Rachel Whitfield"). Find: rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com, VP of People, Vectral Systems.
8. Log audit findings as CRM engagement note using crm_create_engagement (company_ids: ["company_vectral"]). Content includes: all 3 Airtable records still "In Progress" (status update needed), no Linear tracking tickets exist for April moves, no complaints surfaced in email/Slack but post-move accommodation verification needed (ESA housing, ADA accessibility, tight timeline), renewal at_risk with board presentation April 23.
9. Create Linear ticket(s) for follow-up using linear_create_issue. Ticket(s) for ops team to verify: Priya's pet-friendly housing met ESA requirements, Terrence's ADA accessibility confirmed, Camille's tight-timeline delivery completed, and update all 3 Airtable statuses from In Progress to Completed.
10. Send warm check-in email to Rachel Whitfield using send_email (to: rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com, from: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com). Content: asks how Priya, Terrence, and Camille are settling in, offers proactive assistance, does not mention renewal or discount negotiation.
11. Post summary in #customer-engagement using conversations_add_message (channel: C002). Content: Vectral account audit summary for renewal prep - 3 April moves ($15,900), Airtable statuses need updating, no complaints found, Linear tickets created for follow-ups, renewal at_risk with board presentation April 23, check-in email sent to Rachel.

2 The CRM engagement note references that all 3 April relocation Airtable records still show In Progress status and need to be updated to Completed (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The CRM note body explicitly states for each of the three employees: 'Airtable Status: Still marked "In Progress" — NOT updated to Completed.' The overall assessment also says 'All three records are still "In Progress" with no completion confirmation.'
✓Run #2The CRM engagement note body explicitly states for all three moves that 'Airtable status still shows "In Progress" — not updated to Completed' under each employee section, and cross-cutting issue #2 summarizes 'All 3 Airtable records stuck on "In Progress"'.
✓Run #3The CRM engagement note body explicitly states for all three moves that 'Airtable status still "In Progress" — not updated to Completed', making this clearly referenced.
✓Run #4The CRM engagement note body explicitly states 'ALL THREE MOVES STILL IN PROGRESS — NOT YET COMPLETED' and notes that 'All three Airtable relocation records remain in In Progress status.' This directly references the status gap.
✓Run #5The CRM engagement note body explicitly states for each of the 3 employees that their Airtable Status is 'Still In Progress — not updated to Completed', and the cross-cutting issues section further emphasizes 'All three Airtable records still show In Progress — coordinators have not updated statuses to reflect completion.'
✓Run #6The CRM engagement note body explicitly states for all three records: 'Status: IN PROGRESS ⚠️ — not marked as Completed' and 'Airtable status never updated to Completed — needs verification.' The recommended actions also call for updating Airtable status.
3 The CRM engagement note references that no Linear tracking tickets exist for the 3 April moves (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✗✗✗ 50%
✓Run #1The CRM note's Action Items section says '6. Linear tickets created for follow-up,' implying new tickets were created (i.e., none existed before). The note frames ticket creation as a new action, indicating no existing Linear tracking tickets were found for these moves.
✓Run #2The CRM engagement note explicitly states under each move '⚠️ No Linear tickets created' and cross-cutting issue #1 states 'No Linear tickets for any of the 3 moves — older Vectral moves (Kevin Tran, Danielle Osei) had full ticket sets; this wave has zero.'
✓Run #3The CRM engagement note body states 'No Linear tickets were created for this move' for all three relocations (Priya, Terrence, and Camille).
✗Run #4While the agent did call linear_list_issues to check for existing tickets, the CRM engagement note does not reference that no Linear tracking tickets existed for the 3 moves prior to this audit. The note only says 'Linear tickets created for ops follow-up' at the end, not that there was a gap in prior ticket coverage.
✗Run #5The CRM engagement note does not reference that no Linear tracking tickets existed for the 3 April moves. The note describes issues found and action items, but does not call out the absence of Linear tracking tickets as a gap. The agent also never ran linear_list_issues to check for existing tickets.
✗Run #6The CRM engagement note does not explicitly reference that no Linear tracking tickets existed for the 3 April moves. While linear_list_issues returned empty results (confirmed in trajectory), the note only mentions 'Linear tickets created for items 1–5' without noting the absence of pre-existing tickets.
4 The CRM engagement note mentions the Vectral renewal being at_risk and/or the board presentation expected around April 23 (or similar).
outcome

At-risk status is clearly implied.
✓Run #5The CRM engagement note includes a 'RISK TO RENEWAL' section describing the renewal being at risk due to Rachel's discount demands. While 'at_risk' as a status label or 'April 23' are not explicitly used in the note, the agent had retrieved the renewal negotiation engagement (which mentions April 23 board decision) and incorporated the renewal risk context meaningfully into the note.
✓Run #6The CRM engagement note includes a 'CONTEXT FOR DAVID' section explicitly stating 'The renewal negotiation is at final offer stage (10% relocation discount + service sweeteners). Rachel is presenting to her board.' The crm_list_engagements call also revealed the board meeting is April 23, and the note references renewal risk context.

outcome

✓Run #6The email is warm and proactive, focusing on how the employees are settling in and offering support. It contains no mention of 'renewal', 'discount', 'at_risk', or any negotiation-related terms.

10 The #customer-engagement Slack message (channel C002) summarizes the Vectral account status heading into the renewal, referencing at least 2 of: 3 April moves, Airtable status gap, Linear tickets created, email sent to Rachel, or renewal at_risk.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Slack message references all required items: the three April moves, Airtable status gap (all records still 'In Progress'), four Linear tickets created (OPS-397 through OPS-400), email sent to Rachel, and renewal context including the board decision expected April 23 and at_risk churn threat.
